data.title: ACS: Capitalizing on Data-Center Infrastructure Leadership with Underappreciated Upside
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analyst Nicolas Mora upgraded the rating on Actividades de Construccion y Servicios SA to a Buy yesterday, setting a price target of €150.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nicolas Mora has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to ACS’s successful strategic shift toward data‑center related construction and services. Over the past several years, the company has evolved from a relatively overlooked contractor into a key global player in data‑center “pick and shovel” infrastructure, with its Turner unit driving strong growth and margin potential that the market is only partially pricing in.

He also sees meaningful additional upside from expected near‑term earnings revisions, particularly higher EBITDA from data‑center volumes and profitability, along with substantial value creation from a new co‑development joint venture in this segment. On top of that, he highlights further optionality from U.S. managed lanes, infrastructure margin improvement, German infrastructure exposure, Abertis, defense and critical minerals, while noting that theme fatigue around AI/data centers, slower JV ramp‑up and structural complexity are the main risks but do not outweigh the attractive risk‑reward and capital allocation flexibility versus peers.

According to TipRanks, Mora is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 10.5% and a 74.03% success rate.

In another report released on April 10, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €120.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Mineros S.A.: Hemco-Driven Outperformance Supports Buy Rating and Growth Outlook
data.description:
Ben Pirie, an analyst from Atrium Research, maintained the Buy rating on Mineros SA. The associated price target remains the same with C$11.50.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ben Pirie has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Mineros S.A.’s strong operational performance and growth outlook. First-quarter production of gold and silver exceeded his expectations, with total output rising year over year and tracking toward the higher end of the company’s annual guidance range, which supports confidence in near‑term results.

A key driver behind his positive stance is the exceptional performance at the Hemco operation in Nicaragua, where both gold and silver volumes significantly surpassed his forecasts. The ongoing expansion of processing capacity at Hemco, which is already ahead of earlier throughput levels and progressing toward its year‑end target, underpins further production growth and justifies maintaining a Buy recommendation and the existing target price.

data.title: Natera: Premium-Valued Leader in MRD Positioned for Long-Term Growth and Margin Expansion
data.description:
Andrew Brackmann, an analyst from William Blair, has initiated a new Buy rating on Natera (NTRA).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Brackmann has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore Natera’s position as a core long-term growth holding. He highlights the company’s leadership in major diagnostic markets, particularly molecular and minimal residual disease testing, along with a broadening product portfolio that benefits from rising adoption and improving pricing, supporting a path to attractive margin expansion.

He also notes that although Natera trades at a premium valuation versus peers, this is justified by its scale, first-mover advantage in MRD, and substantial long-term earnings potential. Brackmann further points to a large underpenetrated MRD market, a pipeline rich in clinical evidence generation, early detection initiatives, women’s health expansion, and AI partnerships, all of which can unlock additional upside beyond current growth expectations.

In another report released on April 7, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $300.00 price target.

data.title: Tenaya’s TN-401: Differentiated PKP2 Gene Therapy Opportunity Underpins Favorable Risk–Reward and Buy Rating
data.description:
Cory Jubinville, PhD, an analyst from LifeSci Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Tenaya Therapeutics. The associated price target remains the same with $3.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cory Jubinville, PhD has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that center on Tenaya’s differentiated position in PKP2-associated arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy. Feedback from a leading cardiology KOL underscored the substantial unmet need and underdiagnosis in this population, where a meaningful portion of patients in specialized centers could be eligible for gene therapy and currently lack any approved treatment options.

Within this emerging competitive landscape, the KOL viewed Tenaya’s TN-401 as having particularly coherent clinical and biomarker data, with protein expression gains aligning well with improvements in arrhythmic endpoints. Tenaya’s use of mass spectrometry for PKP2 quantification was seen as technically stronger than competing Western blot approaches, which complicates cross-trial comparisons but supports confidence in TN-401’s signal. While acknowledging the small datasets and capital needs, Jubinville appears to believe that Tenaya’s scientific execution, early clinical profile, and positioning in a high-value niche indication justify a favorable risk–reward and support a Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Jubinville, PhD is ranked #1120 out of 12125 analysts.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
